items.title: Why Can’t We Better Prepare for Extreme Weather? | Catherine Nakalembe | TED

translations.zh: Why Can’t We Better Prepare for Extreme Weather? | Catherine Nakalembe | TED

sentences.text: We can predict droughts, floods weeks, even months in advance, yet we still see the same crises unfold.

translations.zh: 我们可以提前数周甚至数月预测干旱、洪水，但我们仍然看到同样的危机发生。

sentences.text: Crop failure, economic and environmental devastation and displacement: the same crises that have trapped famine communities for generations.

translations.zh: 农作物歉收、经济和环境破坏以及流离失所：这些危机使饥荒社区世世代代陷入困境。

sentences.text: This is obviously not a prediction problem, it's a translation problem, one that I came to realize painfully in 2015.

translations.zh: 这显然不是一个预测问题，而是一个翻译问题，我在 2015 年痛苦地认识到了这一问题。

sentences.text: Equipped with the best tools available at the time, including that very expensive fancy drone, I spent August 2015 with my team in Karamoja, documenting yet another failed cropping season, one that I predicted months earlier, using satellite data.

translations.zh: 2015 年 8 月，我和我的团队在卡拉莫贾，配备了当时最好的工具，包括那架非常昂贵的精美无人机，记录了又一个失败的作物季节，这是我几个月前使用卫星数据预测的。

sentences.text: This was part of the worst drought in East Africa in decades, affecting 30 million people in Uganda, Kenya, Somalia and Ethiopia.

translations.zh: 这是东非几十年来最严重干旱的一部分，影响了乌干达、肯尼亚、索马里和埃塞俄比亚的 3000 万人。

sentences.text: After my field work, I did something researchers rarely do.

translations.zh: 实地工作结束后，我做了一些研究人员很少做的事情。

sentences.text: I went straight to the office of the prime minister.

translations.zh: 我直接去了首相办公室。

sentences.text: And 24 hours after my second presentation to several ministers, food trucks were dispatched to Kamagaya on September 26th, 2015, exactly 10 years this week which marked the first time the office used satellite data to trigger an emergency response.

translations.zh: 在我第二次向几位部长发表演讲后 24 小时，2015 年 9 月 26 日，食品卡车被派往镰谷，正好是 10 周年的这一周，这标志着该办公室首次使用卫星数据触发紧急响应。

sentences.text: Following this, I helped design a program that would proactively release financing to support alternative employment for communities affected by drought.

translations.zh: 此后，我帮助设计了一个计划，该计划将主动释放资金，以支持受干旱影响的社区的替代就业。

sentences.text: This program went on to support 450,000 people over five years, saving the government millions in emergency response and deploying several projects that included environmental restoration.

translations.zh: 该计划在五年内为 45 万人提供了支持，为政府节省了数百万美元的应急响应费用，并部署了多个包括环境恢复在内的项目。

sentences.text: But what haunted me then, and is still true today, is this if we could mobilize emergency response within 24 hours, why couldn't we prevent this predictable crisis from unfolding?

translations.zh: 但当时和今天仍然困扰着我的是，如果我们能够在 24 小时内动员紧急响应，为什么我们不能阻止这场可预见的危机的发生呢？

sentences.text: This paradox has deepened because today's capabilities make 2015's best look primitive.

translations.zh: 这个悖论已经加深，因为今天的能力让 2015 年的最佳造型显得很原始。

sentences.text: We have over 8,000 satellites and AI models and computation power that will make predictions, using this data with other data sets, to produce information at unprecedented scales and at unprecedented speeds.

translations.zh: 我们拥有 8,000 多个卫星、人工智能模型和计算能力，可以利用这些数据与其他数据集进行预测，以前所未有的规模和速度生成信息。

sentences.text: Yet, if you can combine this with advances in crop science...

translations.zh: 然而，如果你能将其与作物科学的进步结合起来......

sentences.text: Mobile banking, mechanization, the possibilities seem limitless.

translations.zh: 手机银行、机械化，可能性似乎无限。

sentences.text: Yet just last year, in 2024, nearly one in three people were worried about where their next meal will come from.

translations.zh: 然而就在去年，即 2024 年，近三分之一的人担心下一顿饭从哪里来。

sentences.text: Climate disasters have more than doubled since the 1980s.

translations.zh: 自 20 世纪 80 年代以来，气候灾害增加了一倍多。

sentences.text: So the question is: Why does this keep happening?

translations.zh: 所以问题是：为什么这种情况不断发生？

sentences.text: I would like to tell you a story that will help you bridge the gap between why we have such incredible capabilities and are unable to deliver clear information for a farmer.

translations.zh: 我想告诉您一个故事，它将帮助您弥合为什么我们拥有如此令人难以置信的能力和无法为农民提供清晰信息之间的差距。

sentences.text: For example to increase their yield, save their produce by reducing post-harvest losses,.

translations.zh: 例如，为了提高产量，通过减少收获后损失来保存产品。

sentences.text: And having alternative income so they can survive through tough times.

translations.zh: 并拥有其他收入，以便他们能够度过困难时期。

sentences.text: We have incredible technology But we're missing translators to connect our predictions of that drought, for example, to real, tangible solutions that can get a farmer what they actually need to thrive.

translations.zh: 我们拥有令人难以置信的技术，但我们缺少翻译人员来将我们对干旱的预测与真实、切实的解决方案联系起来，这些解决方案可以为农民提供他们真正需要的繁荣发展。

sentences.text: I'd like to share the story of Mary, whose experience represents millions of smallholder farmers around the world.

translations.zh: 我想分享玛丽的故事，她的经历代表了世界各地数百万小农。

sentences.text: Mary is not her real name, but she's a farmer in Iringa, Tanzania.

translations.zh: 玛丽不是她的真名，但她是坦桑尼亚伊林加的一名农民。

sentences.text: But her story could easily be from Uganda, Madagascar or Senegal, or any other country where smallholders face similar challenges.

translations.zh: 但她的故事很可能来自乌干达、马达加斯加或塞内加尔，或小农面临类似挑战的任何其他国家。

sentences.text: Today's reality is this.

translations.zh: 今天的现实就是这样。

sentences.text: For Mary and her neighbors, they plant February, March, for June, July harvests.

translations.zh: 对于玛丽和她的邻居来说，他们二月、三月播种，六月、七月收获。

sentences.text: This year, Mary acquired improved seeds, along with fertilizer that she heard about from a radio program.

translations.zh: 今年，玛丽获得了改良种子以及她从广播节目中听说的肥料。

sentences.text: Unfortunately, despite her best hopes, rainfall was irregular, and she only harvested 800 kilograms from her one-acre plot.

translations.zh: 不幸的是，尽管她满怀希望，降雨却不稳定，她一英亩的土地只收获了 800 公斤。

sentences.text: Her poultry business that used to provide critical backup income recently collapsed, so she does not have any savings.

translations.zh: 她曾经提供重要后备收入的家禽生意最近倒闭了，所以她没有任何积蓄。

sentences.text: And it's just another year of surviving.

translations.zh: 这只是又一个生存的一年。

sentences.text: Now imagine that Mary did in fact receive seasonal information sometime in January.

translations.zh: 现在想象一下，玛丽确实在一月份的某个时候收到了季节性信息。

sentences.text: Not only did it include the drought prediction, it included when and where she could access fertilizer, a recommended planting date.

translations.zh: 它不仅包括干旱预测，还包括她可以获得肥料的时间和地点以及建议的种植日期。

sentences.text: But most critical is that she has access to financing that she could acquire a water pump so she could irrigate during dry spells.

translations.zh: 但最重要的是，她可以获得融资，购买水泵，以便在干旱期间进行灌溉。

sentences.text: Come July, Mary has 3,000 kilograms.

translations.zh: 到了七月份，玛丽的体重就达到了 3000 公斤。

sentences.text: She has enough to see her through the next harvest, enough income because she has access to buyers who provide premium prices for her produce.

translations.zh: 她有足够的钱度过下一个收获季，足够的收入，因为她可以接触到为她的农产品提供高价的买家。

sentences.text: And storage so she can store it until markets stabilize.

translations.zh: 还有储存，这样她就可以储存它，直到市场稳定为止。

sentences.text: She can send her daughter to school, but most critically, she has extra income, so she can revive her poultry business.

translations.zh: 她可以送女儿上学，但最重要的是，她有额外的收入，这样她就可以重振家禽生意。

sentences.text: This is not science fiction.

translations.zh: 这不是科幻小说。

sentences.text: All the tools, all the technologies to get her that extra income exist today.

translations.zh: 如今，为她带来额外收入的所有工具、所有技术都已存在。

sentences.text: So why is Mary still stuck?

translations.zh: 那么为什么玛丽仍然被困住呢？

sentences.text: Why does she get set back by very predictable crises?

translations.zh: 为什么她会因为可预见的危机而受挫？

sentences.text: The challenge lies in this messy middle, the complex web of relationships and real-life challenges that stand between our incredibly capable predictions and assessments and real, tangible solutions for Mary on the ground.

translations.zh: 挑战就在于这个混乱的中间，复杂的关系网络和现实生活中的挑战，它们介于我们极其强大的预测和评估与玛丽实际的实际、切实的解决方案之间。

sentences.text: For Mary, it's as if all our technology disappears into a black hole.

translations.zh: 对于玛丽来说，就好像我们所有的技术都消失在黑洞中。

sentences.text: And in my experience, drought predictions do not deliver pumps to the ground.

translations.zh: 根据我的经验，干旱预测不会向地面提供水泵。

sentences.text: They produce bulletins.

translations.zh: 他们制作公告。

sentences.text: Add to this complexity the fact that Mary has a small, irregular sized field that doesn't fit our perfect pixels.

translations.zh: 更复杂的是，玛丽有一个小的、不规则大小的区域，不适合我们的完美像素。

sentences.text: We are doing a terrible job mapping fields like Mary's.

translations.zh: 我们在绘制像玛丽这样的领域的工作上做得很糟糕。

sentences.text: In addition to this, the basic infrastructure required for us to improve our predictions and really bring them to the ground are largely missing for regions like where Mary is based.

translations.zh: 除此之外，像玛丽所在的地区基本上缺乏我们改进预测并真正将其付诸实践所需的基础设施。

sentences.text: This complex, messy middle is where all the capabilities shrivel, because it requires things that technology alone cannot provide.

translations.zh: 这个复杂、混乱的中间是所有能力都萎缩的地方，因为它需要的东西是技术本身无法提供的。

sentences.text: For example, it would require partnering with an extension agent who not only delivers fertilizer, trains a farmer and is an excellent data collector, but not replacing them.

translations.zh: 例如，它需要与推广代理合作，该代理不仅提供肥料、培训农民，而且还是优秀的数据收集者，但不能取代他们。

sentences.text: It would also require presenting our information in a way that is accessible to a bank.

translations.zh: 它还需要以银行可以访问的方式提供我们的信息。

sentences.text: So that they can invest in a farmer like Mary, who needs to plant next month.

translations.zh: 这样他们就可以投资像玛丽这样下个月需要种植的农民。

sentences.text: So what is the path forward?

translations.zh: 那么前进的道路是什么？

sentences.text: We can either expand this messy middle, this translation gap, by creating more tech-driven silos, or we can use our current capabilities and venture to connect them to real solutions on the ground.

translations.zh: 我们可以通过创建更多技术驱动的孤岛来扩大这个混乱的中间，这种翻译差距，或者我们可以利用我们当前的能力并冒险将它们与实际的解决方案连接起来。

sentences.text: To do this, there are five fundamental shifts that we would need to do.

translations.zh: 为此，我们需要进行五项根本性转变。

sentences.text: The first is we need to focus on translating, and this would require we're emphasizing reliability over perfection.

translations.zh: 首先，我们需要专注于翻译，这需要我们强调可靠性而不是完美。

sentences.text: A model that is 80 percent accurate, that delivers a pump to Mary, is far better than one that's 90 percent accurate, that never leaves our research paper or dashboard.

translations.zh: 一个准确率为 80% 的模型（为玛丽提供泵）比一个准确度为 90%（永远不会离开我们的研究论文或仪表板）的模型要好得多。

sentences.text: It would require that not only do we fill that critical data gap.

translations.zh: 这不仅要求我们填补关键的数据空白。

sentences.text: So we are better able to predict and assess the conditions in Mary's field, we would need to make sure our predictions can actually be evaluated.

translations.zh: 因此，我们能够更好地预测和评估玛丽领域的条件，我们需要确保我们的预测能够得到实际评估。

items.title: The Attack on Iran — Why Now? | TED Explains the World with Ian Bremmer

translations.zh: The Attack on Iran — Why Now? | TED Explains the World with Ian Bremmer

sentences.text: This morning, after weeks of escalating threats, the United States and Israel bombed several parts of Iran, including the Supreme Leader Ali Ham's compound in Tehran.

translations.zh: 今天早上，经过数周不断升级的威胁，美国和以色列轰炸了伊朗的多个地区，其中包括最高领袖阿里·哈姆在德黑兰的住所。

sentences.text: Iran responded in kind with missiles launched towards Israel and four Gulf Arab countries which host US military bases, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, and the UAE.

translations.zh: 伊朗也向以色列和四个拥有美国军事基地的海湾阿拉伯国家——巴林、科威特、卡塔尔和阿联酋发射了导弹作为回应。

sentences.text: Now, this is a fastmoving situation which feels extraordinarily tenuous.

translations.zh: 现在，形势瞬息万变，让人感觉异常脆弱。

sentences.text: So, we obviously turned immediately to Eurasia Group's founder Ian Bremer to help us make sense of what is happening now and what is to come.

translations.zh: 因此，我们显然立即向欧亚集团创始人伊恩·布雷默寻求帮助，以帮助我们了解现在正在发生的事情以及即将发生的事情。

sentences.text: Ian, hi.

translations.zh: 伊恩，嗨。

sentences.text: Helen, good to see you as always.

translations.zh: 海伦，一如既往地很高兴见到你。

sentences.text: So, Ian, as I mentioned, the threats have been stacking up for weeks now, so this escalation isn't exactly a surprise, but tell us why Trump is doing this now.

translations.zh: 所以，伊恩，正如我所提到的，威胁已经持续数周了，所以这种升级并不令人意外，但请告诉我们特朗普为什么现在这样做。

sentences.text: Well, there are a couple reasons.

translations.zh: 嗯，有几个原因。

sentences.text: First, there is a level of confidence that Trump has both on the back of his success in Venezuela, having removed Nicholas Maduro, not assassinated him, actually bringing him to justice though a lot of Venezuelans and Cubans were killed in the operation.

translations.zh: 首先，特朗普对他在委内瑞拉取得的成功有一定程度的信心，他除掉了尼古拉斯·马杜罗，而不是暗杀了他，实际上将他绳之以法，尽管许多委内瑞拉人和古巴人在行动中丧生。

sentences.text: No Americans were.

translations.zh: 没有美国人。

sentences.text: It's gone over well.

translations.zh: 一切顺利。

sentences.text: The Americans find it popular.

translations.zh: 美国人发现它很受欢迎。

sentences.text: Most of the Latin American countries find it popular.

translations.zh: 大多数拉丁美洲国家都发现它很受欢迎。

sentences.text: And you know he's like well I can do this in other places too.

translations.zh: 你知道他就像我在其他地方也能做到这一点。

sentences.text: So part of it is that he's had experience with Iran before military experience.

translations.zh: 部分原因是他在军事经历之前就曾与伊朗打过交道。

sentences.text: After withdrawing from the Iranian nuclear deal, the JCPOA in his first term he's put the Iranians on notice on a couple of occasions.

translations.zh: 在退出伊朗核协议和联合全面行动计划后，他在第一个任期内曾多次引起伊朗人的注意。

sentences.text: And when he has engaged in military strikes against Iran, the Iranians have not been able or willing to hit him back.

translations.zh: 当他对伊朗进行军事打击时，伊朗人没有能力也不愿意反击他。

sentences.text: And so he doesn't feel like there's a deterrent capability here.

translations.zh: 所以他不觉得这里有威慑能力。

sentences.text: So he's not taking a particularly big risk.

translations.zh: 所以他并没有冒特别大的风险。

sentences.text: Finally he did engage in negotiations but his demands were much greater than the Iranians were willing to engage and that's been clear for months now frankly.

translations.zh: 最后他确实参与了谈判，但他的要求比伊朗人愿意参与的要高得多，坦率地说，这一点几个月来就已经很清楚了。

sentences.text: But he did want to ensure that there was adequate military capabilities in the region both in terms of the strikes that the Americans were going to engage in.

translations.zh: 但他确实希望确保该地区有足够的军事能力来应对美国将要发动的袭击。

sentences.text: That we're now seeing the beginning of but also in terms of America's ability to help defend Israel and its own bases and its allies across the region that final components of that only got into place days ago and then this was the first window of opportunity after that when the Americans and Israelis had a clean shot at the supreme leader and the top military leadership and they gave the order.

translations.zh: 我们现在看到了美国帮助保卫以色列及其基地及其在该地区的盟友的能力的开始，而最终的组成部分几天前才到位，然后这是第一个机会之窗，当时美国和以色列人对最高领导人和最高军事领导层进行了明确的攻击，他们下达了命令。

sentences.text: So that's you know what precipitated all of this.

translations.zh: 所以你知道是什么促成了这一切。

sentences.text: There's a broader question about, you know, how do we feel about the United States exercising unilateral military power all over the world engaging in a policy of regime change, assassinating political leaders.

translations.zh: 有一个更广泛的问题，你知道，我们对美国在世界各地行使单方面军事力量、推行政权更迭政策、暗杀政治领导人有何看法。

sentences.text: What does that mean going forward for the US?

translations.zh: 这对美国的未来意味着什么？

sentences.text: What does it mean for other countries around the world?

translations.zh: 这对世界其他国家意味着什么？

sentences.text: But in terms of what led to this series of strikes, this did not certainly did not surprise us.

translations.zh: 但就导致这一系列罢工的原因而言，这当然并不让我们感到惊讶。

sentences.text: I don't think it was a big surprise to the markets.

translations.zh: 我不认为这对市场来说是一个很大的惊喜。

sentences.text: The scale of the military strikes were greater than were generally anticipated, but nobody credible following this believed that the negotiations were heading for breakthrough.

translations.zh: 军事打击的规模超出了普遍预期，但此后没有任何可信人士相信谈判即将取得突破。

sentences.text: Nor did they believe that this was a taco moment that Trump was going to chicken out.

translations.zh: 他们也不相信特朗普会在这个玉米卷时刻退缩。

sentences.text: They did feel and we did feel that he was going to make good as it were on his threats as were the Israelis.

translations.zh: 他们确实感觉到，我们也确实感觉到，他会像以色列人一样兑现他的威胁。

sentences.text: And they've been coordinating on this very closely for a couple of months now.

translations.zh: 几个月来，他们一直在这方面进行密切协调。

sentences.text: So as you know Trump just announced a few minutes ago on Truth Social that Kam is dead is like what happens now?

translations.zh: 正如你所知，特朗普几分钟前刚刚在 Truth Social 上宣布卡姆已经死了，就像现在发生的情况一样吗？

sentences.text: That feels huge.

translations.zh: 那感觉很大。

sentences.text: I think it is a big deal.

translations.zh: 我认为这是一件大事。

sentences.text: And I mean there are a few things to say about it.

translations.zh: 我的意思是对此有几件事要说。

sentences.text: The first is that let us remember that this is an incredibly brutal regime and that Hamei has not only been personally responsible for the brutality of the regime.

translations.zh: 首先，让我们记住，这是一个令人难以置信的残暴政权，哈梅不仅个人要对该政权的残暴负责。

sentences.text: But also has been a hardliner in terms of refusing effective negotiations with the Americans over the past months.

translations.zh: 但在过去几个月里，他在拒绝与美国人进行有效谈判方面一直表现强硬。

sentences.text: I mean certainly many thousands of Iranian civilians and believed tens of thousands of Iranian civilians were killed under the order of Hamei in January when they were demonstrating and most of these were unarmed.

translations.zh: 我的意思是，肯定有成千上万的伊朗平民，相信有数万名伊朗平民在一月份示威时在哈梅的命令下被杀害，其中大多数人手无寸铁。

sentences.text: And not posing a direct security threat to the troops that they were facing off against and that is frankly we haven't seen that kind of short-term brutality in the region anywhere.

translations.zh: 并且不会对他们所面对的部队构成直接的安全威胁，坦率地说，我们在该地区的任何地方都没有看到这种短期的暴行。

sentences.text: You'd have to look to Sudan, you'd have to look to Rwanda before you saw that kind of killing.

translations.zh: 在看到这种杀戮之前，你必须看看苏丹，你必须看看卢旺达。

sentences.text: You're talking about the sort of deaths over the course of a couple weeks that you saw in Gaza over an entire year of fighting.

translations.zh: 你说的是在加沙一整年的战斗中几周内发生的死亡人数。

sentences.text: So that's really an extraordinary brutality and as a consequence, there aren't a lot of people that are going to shed a tear over the death of Hamei.

translations.zh: 所以这确实是一种非同寻常的暴行，因此，没有多少人会为哈美的死流泪。

sentences.text: Having said that, he is not the regime.

translations.zh: 话虽如此，他不是政权。

sentences.text: He's 86 years old.

translations.zh: 他今年 86 岁了。

sentences.text: They've been planning for transition for a long time.

translations.zh: 他们长期以来一直在计划转型。

sentences.text: The fact that he has been killed will make him seem to be more of a martyr in the eyes certainly of those that support the regime inside Iran.

translations.zh: 他被杀的事实将使他在那些支持伊朗境内政权的人眼中更像是一位烈士。

sentences.text: Of whom there are many a minimum of probably 15 and 20% of the population that are hardened regime supporters and will become more so on the back of this assassination.

translations.zh: 其中至少有 15% 到 20% 的人是顽固的政权支持者，并且在这次暗杀事件后将变得更加坚定。

sentences.text: The Iranian military leadership has taken substantial strikes over the 12-day war last year and they reconstituted themselves quite quickly.

translations.zh: 伊朗军方领导层在去年为期12天的战争中遭受了重大打击，并且很快就进行了重组。

sentences.text: It didn't affect their ability and willingness to repress internally.

translations.zh: 这并不影响他们内心压抑的能力和意愿。

sentences.text: It does appear that a number of other military leaders may well be dead probably not the president but others.

translations.zh: 看来其他一些军事领导人很可能已经去世，可能不是总统，而是其他人。

sentences.text: The president's not a very important figure, frankly, in the eyes of the Iranian people or in driving power, but the head of the IRGC may well be dead.

translations.zh: 坦率地说，在伊朗人民眼中或在推动权力方面，总统并不是一个非常重要的人物，但伊斯兰革命卫队的领导人很可能已经去世了。

sentences.text: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, the Israelis have announced that he has been killed as well as other top adviserss to the Supreme Leader on the military side.

translations.zh: 以色列伊斯兰革命卫队宣布，他以及最高领袖军事方面的其他高级顾问均已被杀。

sentences.text: But it's very far to go from that to it's the end of the regime because remember when Trump launched these strikes with the Israelis, he said, "We're attacking all of the military installations."

translations.zh: 但距离该政权的终结还有很长的路要走，因为记得特朗普对以色列发动这些袭击时，他说，“我们正在攻击所有军事设施。”

sentences.text: And they've certainly done that.

translations.zh: 他们确实做到了。

sentences.text: They're attacking all the ballistic missile installations that they can find that they can get to.

translations.zh: 他们正在攻击所有他们能到达的弹道导弹设施。

sentences.text: They're certainly doing that.

translations.zh: 他们当然正在这样做。

sentences.text: But he also said this is meant to be an operation of regime change.

translations.zh: 但他也表示，这意味着政权更迭的行动。

sentences.text: She said, "Now that we're taking out the leadership, it is your once in a generation opportunity, the Iranian people, to take your government.

translations.zh: 她说：“现在我们要推翻领导权，伊朗人民，这是千载难逢的机会来夺取你们的政府。

sentences.text: But taking the government requires overthrowing those members of the IRGC, the military, the bases, the paramilitary groups, the police officers on the streets.

translations.zh: 但夺取政府需要推翻伊斯兰革命卫队成员、军队、基地、准军事团体和街头警察。

items.title: The Case for Spending More Time with Your Friends | Rhaina Cohen | TED

translations.zh: The Case for Spending More Time with Your Friends | Rhaina Cohen | TED

sentences.text: For a lot of us, this is a moment when questions of family come into sharper focus who do we gather with who supports us who do we choose?

translations.zh: 对于我们很多人来说，这是一个家庭问题更加引人注目的时刻，我们与谁聚集在一起，谁支持我们，我们选择谁？

sentences.text: And who chooses us and for some of us, those answers might feel wonderfully clear.

translations.zh: 谁选择了我们，对于我们中的一些人来说，这些答案可能会感觉非常清楚。

sentences.text: But for others, there might be a tenderness around this or a sense that your answers aren't quite what tradition tells you they should be.

translations.zh: 但对其他人来说，可能会对此感到温柔，或者感觉你的答案并不完全是传统告诉你的答案。

sentences.text: And that's why our guest today feels especially essential right now.

translations.zh: 这就是为什么我们今天的客人现在感觉特别重要。

sentences.text: She's a journalist and author of the other significant others.

translations.zh: 她是一名记者，也是其他重要人物的作者。

sentences.text: And last year, she delivered a TED talk that pushed us to expand who we count as our people and to recognize the deep sustaining connections that don't always fit neatly onto holiday cards or family trees.

translations.zh: 去年，她发表了一场 TED 演讲，促使我们扩大我们的员工范围，并认识到深层的维持联系并不总是完全适合节日贺卡或家谱。

sentences.text: But can shape our lives in very profound ways.

translations.zh: 但可以以非常深刻的方式塑造我们的生活。

sentences.text: I'm so grateful she's here to help us reflect on belonging, connection, and relationships that can carry us into this season and well beyond it.

translations.zh: 我非常感谢她在这里帮助我们反思归属感、联系和关系，这些可以让我们进入这个季节并超越它。

sentences.text: Please give a welcome to Raina Cohen.

translations.zh: 请欢迎雷娜·科恩。

sentences.text: What a nice intro.

translations.zh: 多好的介绍啊。

sentences.text: Thanks, Whitney.

translations.zh: 谢谢，惠特尼。

sentences.text: Well, I think just maybe to kick things off, your talk, the talk that you delivered on the TED stage is sort of a distillation of the big idea from your.

translations.zh: 好吧，我想也许首先，你的演讲，你在 TED 舞台上发表的演讲是你的伟大想法的升华。

sentences.text: Book, The Other Significant Others that essentially argues for a more expansive approach to friendship and the role it can play for all of us.

translations.zh: 《其他重要的其他人》一书本质上主张对友谊采取更广泛的态度，以及它可以为我们所有人发挥的作用。

sentences.text: And to pull a quote from the book, you say, "If we don't limit friendship, it can be central to our lives."

translations.zh: 引用书中的话，你说：“如果我们不限制友谊，它就会成为我们生活的核心。”

sentences.text: So, for those of us who aren't familiar, as a primer, I think for our conversation, what do you mean by this?

translations.zh: 那么，对于我们这些不熟悉的人来说，作为入门，我想对于我们的谈话，你这是什么意思？

sentences.text: How are we limiting friendship?

translations.zh: 我们如何限制友谊？

sentences.text: And how could we think about it more fully?

translations.zh: 我们怎样才能更全面地思考这个问题？

sentences.text: I think we limit friendship by thinking it can do less than it actually can.

translations.zh: 我认为我们限制了友谊，因为我们认为友谊所能做的事情比实际做的要少。

sentences.text: And one of the ways that feels really clear is by looking across time and across places and seeing what friendship looked like in those times and places.

translations.zh: 感觉真正清晰的方法之一就是跨越时间和地点，看看友谊在那些时间和地点是什么样子。

sentences.text: And it was different.

translations.zh: 但情况有所不同。

sentences.text: For one thing, I think the expectation now is that friendship is going to be a relationship that's private.

translations.zh: 一方面，我认为现在的期望是友谊将成为一种私人关系。

sentences.text: It's just between you and someone else or maybe it's a small group of people and it is a nice addition to your life but it is not an essential part of your life.

translations.zh: 它只是在你和其他人之间，或者可能是一小群人之间，它是你生活的一个很好的补充，但它不是你生活的重要组成部分。

sentences.text: It's something that you can you know maybe put on the back burner and that was just not true in many other places.

translations.zh: 你知道，这件事可能会被搁置一旁，但在许多其他地方并非如此。

sentences.text: I mean you can see that there were rituals that were built around recognizing friendship potentially for the rest of your life.

translations.zh: 我的意思是，你可以看到，有一些仪式是围绕着认识到你的余生可能存在的友谊而建立的。

sentences.text: There an example of this ritual is called sworn brotherhood.

translations.zh: 这种仪式的一个例子被称为宣誓兄弟会。

sentences.text: And it was recognized as something that people would witness like that commitment.

translations.zh: 它被认为是人们会见证的事情，就像这样的承诺。

sentences.text: We don't think about friendship as involving commitment let alone a commitment that you are going to formalize in front of other people.

translations.zh: 我们不认为友谊涉及承诺，更不用说您将在其他人面前正式做出的承诺。

sentences.text: So I think even the idea of commitment is something that would surprise a lot of people.

translations.zh: 所以我认为即使是承诺的想法也会让很多人感到惊讶。

sentences.text: And then I think the kinds of feelings that we think are possible to experience in friendship are also kind of limited.

translations.zh: 然后我认为我们认为在友谊中可能体验到的感受也是有限的。

sentences.text: That we hold out romantic relationships as the relationship where you can experience excitement and infatuation.

translations.zh: 我们将浪漫关系视为可以体验兴奋和迷恋的关系。

sentences.text: And the you know the sort of fluttery feelings that actually friends can experience.

translations.zh: 你知道真正的朋友可以体验到的那种激动的感觉。

sentences.text: I can you know can talk about my own experience with this.

translations.zh: 我可以告诉你可以谈谈我自己的经历吗？

sentences.text: It's partly what led me to write the book.

translations.zh: 这也是我写这本书的部分原因。

sentences.text: But again if you look across different times and places you will see that kind of affection where friends would talk about loving each other and that's that was obvious that you would love your friends.

translations.zh: 但是，如果你看看不同的时间和地点，你会看到那种朋友们谈论彼此相爱的感情，这很明显你会爱你的朋友。

sentences.text: And I think especially you know we're talking about certain subsets of society it's not that common to talk in that language of love or to snuggle up to your friend without that being a question of you know are you romantically involved.

translations.zh: 我认为，尤其是你知道我们正在谈论社会的某些子集，用爱的语言交谈或依偎在你的朋友身上而不是你知道你是否陷入浪漫的问题并不常见。

sentences.text: So I think between the idea that friendship is private that it doesn't it should be easy, it shouldn't necessarily involve commitment.

translations.zh: 所以我认为，友谊是私人的，但它并不应该是容易的，它不一定需要承诺。

sentences.text: And that there are there's kind of a cap on how intense the emotional experience might be.

translations.zh: 而且情感体验的强度是有上限的。

sentences.text: All of those are ways that we I think underestimate what friendship can be and do for our lives.

translations.zh: 我认为所有这些都是我们低估了友谊的意义和对我们生活的影响。

sentences.text: And you mentioned just now that you have sort of a personal connection to this.

translations.zh: 您刚才提到您与此有某种个人联系。

sentences.text: Can you tell us a little bit more about why you wrote the book and then ultimately gave the talk?

translations.zh: 您能告诉我们更多关于您写这本书并最终发表演讲的原因吗？

sentences.text: So, I found myself in a friendship that really broke the definition of friendship that I thought was, you know, what it was supposed to be.

translations.zh: 所以，我发现自己处于一种友谊之中，这种友谊真的打破了我所认为的友谊的定义，你知道，它应该是什么。

sentences.text: And, this happened in my early 20s and I ended up meeting a friend who, it turned out lived 5 minutes, a five-minute walk away from me.

translations.zh: 这件事发生在我 20 岁出头的时候，我最终遇到了一位朋友，结果他住的地方离我只有 5 分钟路程，步行 5 分钟即可到达。

sentences.text: And we became much more intertwined in each other's lives, than I had ex I had experienced maybe something somewhat comparable.

translations.zh: 我们在彼此的生活中变得更加交织在一起，比我的前任经历过的事情也许有些类似。

sentences.text: A couple of times, but we found ourselves struggling with even the language of best friend that felt inadequate that something more like partners maybe fit.

translations.zh: 有几次，我们发现自己甚至在最好朋友的语言上都感到挣扎，这种语言感觉不够，更像是伴侣的东西可能更适合。

sentences.text: And I found myself thinking about other people that I had known at different points in my life who had these really intense, devoted friendships that didn't kind of couldn't be captured with the term friend or best friend.

translations.zh: 我发现自己想到了我在生命中不同阶段认识的其他人，他们拥有真正强烈、忠诚的友谊，这种友谊不能用朋友或最好的朋友这个词来形容。

sentences.text: And then I looked to history, some of what I was just talking about before, and realized that we have a really specific idea about what friendship is now.

translations.zh: 然后我回顾了历史，回顾了我之前谈到的一些历史，并意识到我们现在对于友谊是什么有一个非常具体的想法。

sentences.text: And I wanted to find people today who had these kinds of friendships.

translations.zh: 今天我想找到拥有这种友谊的人。

sentences.text: And essentially over the course of those interviews, I realized that this wasn't just a project to lift up and help people recognize that there were these extremely close friendships that I called Platonic partnerships.

translations.zh: 本质上，在这些采访的过程中，我意识到这不仅仅是一个提升和帮助人们认识到存在着我称之为柏拉图式伙伴关系的极其亲密的友谊的项目。

sentences.text: But that because they challenged the definition of friendship by blending together kinds of elements of partnership and platonic friendship that they ended up asking all these bigger questions about how do we, you know,.

translations.zh: 但因为他们通过将伙伴关系和柏拉图式友谊的各种元素融合在一起来挑战友谊的定义，所以他们最终提出了所有这些更大的问题，即我们如何，你知道的。

sentences.text: Know that a romantic relationship is romantic?

translations.zh: 知道恋爱关系很浪漫吗？

sentences.text: Why are romantic relationships held up higher than friendships by default?

translations.zh: 为什么默认情况下浪漫关系比友谊更受重视？

sentences.text: Like how do we define what family is?

translations.zh: 比如我们如何定义什么是家庭？

sentences.text: How does the state define what an important relationship is?

translations.zh: 国家如何定义什么是重要关系？

sentences.text: So basically like it started from this personal place, but all these bigger questions came up for me that I wanted to tackle in a book.

translations.zh: 所以基本上就像是从这个个人的地方开始的，但所有这些更大的问题都出现在我面前，我想在一本书中解决。

sentences.text: And I think it's really important to note that this isn't an argument against romantic relationships.

translations.zh: 我认为值得注意的是，这并不是反对浪漫关系的论点。

sentences.text: You're not suggesting that you have either or, right?

translations.zh: 你并不是说你有或者，对吧？

sentences.text: No.

translations.zh: 不。

sentences.text: Absolutely not.

translations.zh: 绝对不是。

sentences.text: I mean, I try to be really careful in a number of ways to say that it's not either or.

translations.zh: 我的意思是，我尝试在很多方面非常小心地说这不是非此即彼。

sentences.text: I think that would be kind of using the same logic that I am trying to push against the idea that there's one right way to live.

translations.zh: 我认为这与我试图反对“只有一种正确的生活方式”这一观点的逻辑相同。

items.title: How Competition Is Stifling AI Breakthroughs | Llion Jones | TED

translations.zh: How Competition Is Stifling AI Breakthroughs | Llion Jones | TED

sentences.text: So, as mentioned, I'm probably most well-known as one of the transformers authors.

translations.zh: 因此，如前所述，我可能是最知名的变形金刚作者之一。

sentences.text: Transformers are, of course, the T in ChatGPT, and are the architectures that run most of the state-of-the-art artificial intelligence if I think back to that time when we were working on the transformers, I remember it as a very organic.

translations.zh: 当然，Transformers 是 ChatGPT 中的 T，并且是运行大多数最先进人工智能的架构，如果我回想起我们开发 Transformer 的时候，我记得它是一个非常有机的。

sentences.text: Bottom-up kind of project, where the idea came from talking over lunch or scribbling randomly on the whiteboards in the office.

translations.zh: 自下而上的项目，其想法来自于午餐时的交谈或在办公室的白板上随意涂鸦。

sentences.text: And importantly, when we felt like we did actually have a good idea, we had the freedom to actually spend the time and go and work on it.

translations.zh: 重要的是，当我们觉得自己确实有一个好主意时，我们就可以自由地花时间去实现它。

sentences.text: And even more importantly, we didn't have any pressure that was coming down from management.

translations.zh: 更重要的是，我们没有任何来自管理层的压力。

sentences.text: No pressure to work on any particular project, publish a number of papers, to push a certain number up.

translations.zh: 没有压力去从事任何特定的项目、发表大量论文、推动一定数量的上升。

sentences.text: So that's the image I want you to have in your mind, right?

translations.zh: 这就是我希望你脑海中浮现的形象，对吧？

sentences.text: That is the kind of environment that allowed the transformer to come into existence.

translations.zh: 这就是变压器诞生的环境。

sentences.text: An organic, open-ended and with a lot of freedom to pursue the ideas that we thought were interesting and important.

translations.zh: 一个有机的、开放式的，有很大的自由来追求我们认为有趣和重要的想法。

sentences.text: And my deep concern is that right now in the AI industry, we do not have this kind of environment.

translations.zh: 而我深感担忧的是，目前在人工智能行业，我们还没有这样的环境。

sentences.text: And I want to talk about why not and what can we do about it so the main paradox that I see in artificial intelligence research, or the industry in general right now.

translations.zh: 我想谈谈为什么不这样做以及我们能做些什么，这是我在人工智能研究或目前整个行业中看到的主要悖论。

sentences.text: Is that despite the fact that there's never been so much interest and resources and money and talent, this has somehow caused a narrowing of the research that we're doing.

translations.zh: 尽管事实上从来没有如此多的兴趣、资源、金钱和人才，但这在某种程度上导致了我们正在进行的研究范围的缩小。

sentences.text: And to me, I think the reason is fairly obvious.

translations.zh: 对我来说，我认为原因相当明显。

sentences.text: It's because the immense amount of pressure that comes with that, right?

translations.zh: 因为随之而来的压力是巨大的，对吗？

sentences.text: Pressure from investors that are going to ask for a return on their investment and pressure that comes from individuals because this is such an overcrowded industry right now, where it is very difficult to stand out.

translations.zh: 来自要求投资回报的投资者的压力和来自个人的压力，因为目前这是一个过度拥挤的行业，很难脱颖而出。

sentences.text: And the researchers are really feeling this pressure, right if you're doing, let's say, standard AI research right now, you kind of have to assume that there's maybe three or four other groups doing something very similar or maybe exactly the same.

translations.zh: 研究人员确实感受到了这种压力，如果你现在正在进行标准人工智能研究，你就必须假设可能还有三四个其他小组在做非常相似或完全相同的事情。

sentences.text: So you have to spend the time checking to see if you've been scooped, to see if someone else has put your idea out there.

translations.zh: 所以你必须花时间检查你是否被抢先，看看其他人是否已经提出了你的想法。

sentences.text: And even in academia, where you would hope you would have more freedom, there's pressure to publish, right, and to have your papers published.

translations.zh: 即使在学术界，你希望自己有更多的自由，也有发表论文的压力，对吧，发表你的论文。

sentences.text: So if you have an interesting idea that could create something very interesting, or you have kind of a mediocre idea that it'll probably get a paper and probably get accepted, the temptation is to go for the low-hanging fruit.

translations.zh: 因此，如果你有一个有趣的想法，可以创造出一些非常有趣的东西，或者你有一个平庸的想法，它可能会得到一篇论文并可能被接受，那么诱惑就是去追求唾手可得的果实。

sentences.text: So unfortunately, this pressure damages the science, because people are rushing out papers, and it's reducing the amount of creativity that we have.

translations.zh: 不幸的是，这种压力损害了科学，因为人们急于发表论文，这减少了我们的创造力。

sentences.text: So I want to take an analogy from AI itself.

translations.zh: 所以我想用人工智能本身来类比。

sentences.text: So when we're designing AI search algorithms, we have to trade off something called the exploration-exploitation trade off when you're searching for a solution, you can either spend your time exploring or exploiting if you spend all your time exploring, then you will probably only find a large number of medical solutions if you spend your time just exploiting, then you might lose out on finding other alternative solutions that you might be able to exploit better and improve better.

translations.zh: 所以当我们设计人工智能搜索算法时，当你寻找解决方案时，我们必须权衡一种叫做探索-利用权衡的东西，你要么花时间探索，要么利用，如果你把所有的时间都花在探索上，那么你可能只会找到大量的医疗解决方案，如果你把时间花在探索上，那么你可能会失去寻找其他你可能能够更好地利用和改进的替代解决方案。

sentences.text: And we are almost certainly in that situation right now in the AI industry.

translations.zh: 几乎可以肯定，我们现在的人工智能行业就处于这种情况。

sentences.text: So all I really want to ask you today is to consider just changing that balance a little bit, right?

translations.zh: 所以我今天真正想问你的是考虑稍微改变一下这种平衡，对吧？

sentences.text: Just turning up the dial and exploring more.

translations.zh: 只需转动旋钮并探索更多。

sentences.text: So I actually remember what it was like just before the transformers, and I want to paint that picture for you as well.

translations.zh: 所以我实际上还记得《变形金刚》之前的样子，我也想为你画出那幅画。

sentences.text: Back then, my main memory was there were a lot of papers coming out and they were always permutating the current architecture which was recurrent neural networks at the time, just endlessly trying different things, different gates, different layers,.

translations.zh: 当时，我的主要记忆是有很多论文发表，它们总是对当前的架构进行排列，当时的架构是循环神经网络，只是无休止地尝试不同的东西，不同的门，不同的层。

sentences.text: Mostly for incremental gains.

translations.zh: 主要是为了增量收益。

sentences.text: And then after the transformer came out, all of that work that was spent on improving the recurrent neural network kind of felt a bit pointless.

translations.zh: 在 Transformer 出现之后，所有花在改进循环神经网络上的工作都感觉有点毫无意义。

sentences.text: Maybe that's a bit too harsh, but think of it like this.

translations.zh: 也许这有点太严厉了，但是这样想吧。

sentences.text: How much time do you think those researchers would have spent trying to improve the recurrent neural network if they knew something like transformers was around the corner, right?

translations.zh: 如果这些研究人员知道像变形金刚这样的东西即将出现，你认为他们会花多少时间来尝试改进循环神经网络，对吗？

sentences.text: It turned out we needed a longer conceptual leap.

translations.zh: 事实证明，我们需要一个更长的概念飞跃。

sentences.text: We needed to throw away recurrence completely and again, I am worried that we're in that situation right now where we're just concentrating on one architecture.

translations.zh: 我们需要一次又一次地彻底放弃重复，我担心我们现在正处于那种只专注于一种架构的情况。

sentences.text: And just permuting it and trying different things where there might be a breakthrough just around the corner.

translations.zh: 只要改变它并尝试不同的事情，也许突破就在眼前。

sentences.text: And if there is, then we should be acting like it.

translations.zh: 如果有的话，那么我们就应该这样做。

sentences.text: The next breakthrough, almost by definition, has to come from this sort of open-ended, much more speculative research, right?

translations.zh: 几乎按照定义，下一个突破必须来自这种开放式、更具推测性的研究，对吗？

sentences.text: And the only way to really hedge your bets against missing out on the next big thing is to invest in this kind of research.

translations.zh: 真正避免错过下一件大事的唯一方法就是投资此类研究。

sentences.text: So if I came up here and did nothing but just moan about the current situation, I don't think it would be a great talk.

translations.zh: 因此，如果我来到这里除了抱怨当前的状况之外什么也没做，我认为这不会是一次很好的演讲。

sentences.text: So I want to give you a couple of suggestions.

translations.zh: 所以我想给你一些建议。

sentences.text: First of all, in my company, we champion having nature-inspired.

translations.zh: 首先，在我的公司，我们倡导以自然为灵感。

sentences.text: So there are still things, plenty of things that the human brain can do, that current state-of-the-art AI can't do.

translations.zh: 因此，仍然有很多事情是人类大脑可以做的，而当前最先进的人工智能却无法做到的。

sentences.text: So maybe if we take some inspiration from nature, we can get some of those properties.

translations.zh: 因此，如果我们从大自然中获得一些灵感，也许我们可以获得其中的一些特性。

sentences.text: But that's kind of my bias.

translations.zh: 但这是我的偏见。

sentences.text: You should follow what's interesting to you?

translations.zh: 你应该关注你感兴趣的事情？

sentences.text: There's actually a quote I heard two weeks ago and I thought, that's perfect, I'm having that for my talk.

translations.zh: 事实上，我两周前听到过一句话，我想，这太完美了，我就用它来做我的演讲。

sentences.text: And I think I'm stealing it from a guy called Brian Chung.

translations.zh: 我想我是从一个叫 Brian Chung 的人那里偷来的。

sentences.text: And it goes like this.

translations.zh: 事情是这样的。

sentences.text: “You should only do research that wouldn’t happen if you weren’t working on it.

translations.zh: “你应该只做那些如果你不努力就不会发生的研究。

sentences.text: ” And I think that captures it perfectly.

translations.zh: ” 我认为这完美地体现了这一点。

sentences.text: And if we all did that, we wouldn’t be stepping on each other’s toes, and we'd be exploring much more efficiently.

translations.zh: 如果我们都这样做，我们就不会踩到彼此的脚趾，我们的探索也会更加有效。

sentences.text: So I want to give you a concrete example.

translations.zh: 所以我想给大家举一个具体的例子。

sentences.text: There's a piece of research that we put out recently called the Continuous Thought Machine.

translations.zh: 我们最近推出了一项名为“连续思维机器”的研究。

sentences.text: And all we did is we just took a little bit of inspiration from nature.

translations.zh: 我们所做的只是从大自然中获取一点灵感。

sentences.text: So in the human brain, synchronization is very important.

translations.zh: 所以在人脑中，同步是非常重要的。

sentences.text: And we try to add this kind of synchronization into artificial neural networks.

translations.zh: 我们尝试将这种同步添加到人工神经网络中。

sentences.text: I remember the employee coming to me with the idea and I said, OK, work on it for a week, and we’ll see what happens that employee later confided in me that in his previous employment, or even in his academic position before that he probably would have gotten skepticism and told not to waste his time.

translations.zh: 我记得那位员工带着这个想法来找我，我说，好吧，研究一周，我们会看看会发生什么，这位员工后来向我透露，在他以前的工作中，甚至在他之前的学术职位上，他可能会受到怀疑，并被告知不要浪费时间。

sentences.text: But after that week, he started to find much more interesting properties of this model.

translations.zh: 但在那一周之后，他开始发现这个模型更有趣的特性。

sentences.text: And the project became a success.

translations.zh: 该项目取得了成功。

sentences.text: We actually announced that we got a spotlight at NeurIPS this year.

translations.zh: 事实上，我们宣布今年我们在 NeurIPS 上受到了关注。

sentences.text: And I think there's a couple of reasons for that.

translations.zh: 我认为这有几个原因。

items.title: TED Idea Search — 9 Cities, 1 Main Stage | Premiering Tuesday, March 3

translations.zh: TED Idea Search — 9 Cities, 1 Main Stage | Premiering Tuesday, March 3

sentences.text: My name is [music] Reggie Watts and my life changed forever when I got the opportunity to speak at TED.

translations.zh: 我的名字是[音乐]雷吉·瓦茨，当我有机会在 TED 演讲时，我的生活永远改变了。

sentences.text: Now TED is searching the globe for ideas [music] that could change everything.

translations.zh: 现在，TED 正在全球范围内寻找可以改变一切的想法（音乐）。

sentences.text: Nine TED X events around the world.

translations.zh: 全球九场 TED X 活动。

sentences.text: Giving a global [music] voice to the unknown.

translations.zh: 但这还不是全部。

sentences.text: Coached by an expert panel, one lucky speaker will be surprised with the opportunity of a lifetime.

translations.zh: 在专家小组的指导下，一位幸运的演讲者将惊讶地发现一生难得的机会。

sentences.text: A chance to [music] speak on the TED Main stage and have their voices heard by millions.

translations.zh: 有机会在 TED 主舞台上[音乐]演讲，让数百万人听到他们的声音。

sentences.text: It's just so exciting.

translations.zh: 真是太令人兴奋了。

sentences.text: Wow.

translations.zh: 哇。

sentences.text: This is exactly the [music] opportunity to reach out to millions of people.

translations.zh: 这正是接触数百万人的[音乐]机会。

sentences.text: It's the biggest stage in the world.

translations.zh: 这是世界上最大的舞台。

sentences.text: It's bigger than [music] any dream.

translations.zh: 它比[音乐]任何梦想都伟大。

sentences.text: There were a bunch of people that were reading their scripts.

translations.zh: 有一群人正在阅读他们的剧本。

sentences.text: Small punch lines.

translations.zh: 小妙语。

sentences.text: I think there's a lot of work to be done.

translations.zh: 我认为还有很多工作要做。

sentences.text: No clip notes.

translations.zh: 没有剪辑笔记。

sentences.text: Can I start again?

translations.zh: 我可以重新开始吗？

sentences.text: This I cannot.

translations.zh: 这个我不能。

sentences.text: It blew me away.

translations.zh: 它让我大吃一惊。

sentences.text: Wow.

translations.zh: 哇。

sentences.text: I'm really sorry.

translations.zh: 我真的很抱歉。

sentences.text: So big.
