sentences.text: This is the TED idea search.

translations.zh: 这就是 TED 创意搜索。

items.title: What Happened When I Started Measuring My Life Every Day | Chris Musser | TED

translations.zh: What Happened When I Started Measuring My Life Every Day | Chris Musser | TED

sentences.text: Can I get a show of hands if you use a wearable?

translations.zh: 如果您使用可穿戴设备，我可以举手吗？

sentences.text: Fitbit, Oura, Apple Watch, track your health, come on.

translations.zh: Fitbit、Oura、Apple Watch，追踪您的健康状况，加油。

sentences.text: OK, it's like 50 percent of you.

translations.zh: 好吧，这就像你的 50% 一样。

sentences.text: I love this so my question for us today, for you but also the non-wearables, is what if we expanded from not just measuring our health but to measuring all the important parts of our lives?

translations.zh: 我喜欢这个，所以今天我向我们、你们以及非可穿戴设备提出的问题是，如果我们不仅测量我们的健康状况，而且扩展到测量我们生活的所有重要部分，会怎么样？

sentences.text: So this really started for me right after I turned 25, and I started to wake up in the middle of the night pretty consistently with these panics.

translations.zh: 所以这对我来说真的是在我 25 岁之后就开始了，我开始经常在半夜醒来，伴随着这些恐慌。

sentences.text: A lot of folks wake up with to-do lists bouncing through their minds.

translations.zh: 很多人醒来时脑子里都会浮现出待办事项清单。

sentences.text: A lot of you maybe, some of you?

translations.zh: 也许你们很多人，你们中的一些人？

sentences.text: I had to-do lists, but I also had this major to-be list.

translations.zh: 我有待办事项清单，但我也有这个主要的待办事项清单。

sentences.text: Really just will I be a good man, will I live a good life?

translations.zh: 我真的会做一个好人，过上美好的生活吗？

sentences.text: And now, I'm a pretty insecure guy generally.

translations.zh: 现在，我基本上是一个非常没有安全感的人。

sentences.text: But I have this thing, I have one of those extremely encouraging moms which maybe some of you have.

translations.zh: 但我有这样的事情，我有一位非常鼓舞人心的妈妈，也许你们中的一些人也有这样的妈妈。

sentences.text: Mine is very likely more encouraging.

translations.zh: 我的很可能更令人鼓舞。

sentences.text: And so, with her voice in my mind, I would basically answer the question, yeah, I am going to live a good life.

translations.zh: 因此，当我脑海中浮现出她的声音时，我基本上会回答这个问题：是的，我会过上美好的生活。

sentences.text: In fact, maybe she's right, I will change millions of lives.

translations.zh: 事实上，也许她是对的，我将改变数百万人的生活。

sentences.text: Maybe I'll be president.

translations.zh: 也许我会成为总统。

sentences.text: So I had this narrative from 25 to about 33, and then I hit 33.

translations.zh: 所以我从 25 到 33 左右都有这个故事，然后我就到了 33。

sentences.text: And I haven't changed that many lives.

translations.zh: 我并没有改变那么多人的生活。

sentences.text: And I find out one day that Bill Clinton was already a governor by 33, which to most of you is no big deal.

translations.zh: 有一天，我发现比尔·克林顿 (Bill Clinton) 33 岁时已经成为州长，这对你们大多数人来说没什么大不了的。

sentences.text: But ask my wife, Ashley, that broke me.

translations.zh: 但问问我的妻子阿什利，这让我心碎。

sentences.text: I think what happened is it kind of shattered this, sort of, mom-induced megalomania I had, and it made me take an actual look at my life.

translations.zh: 我认为发生的事情打破了我这种由妈妈引发的狂妄自大，它让我真正审视自己的生活。

sentences.text: And so I looked at my professional life, but then what about my friendships?

translations.zh: 所以我审视了我的职业生涯，那么我的友谊又如何呢？

sentences.text: What about my contributions to society, what about my character?

translations.zh: 我对社会的贡献怎么样，我的品格怎么样？

sentences.text: And honestly, I looked and I was fine, but I wasn't thriving, I wasn't flourishing.

translations.zh: 老实说，我看起来很好，但我并没有蓬勃发展，我没有蓬勃发展。

sentences.text: And so then it struck me, you know, Chris, a lot of people don't live good lives.

translations.zh: 然后我突然意识到，克里斯，你知道，很多人都过着不好的生活。

sentences.text: Why do you think you would automatically be any different?

translations.zh: 为什么你认为你会自动变得不同？

sentences.text: What if I wake up in 50 years and I discover I have not become the man I wanted to be.

translations.zh: 如果 50 年后我醒来发现自己没有成为我想成为的人怎么办？

sentences.text: I'm guessing some of you have similar thoughts, because Gallup tells us that only 33 percent of us are thriving in life.

translations.zh: 我猜你们中的一些人也有类似的想法，因为盖洛普告诉我们，只有 33% 的人在生活中蒸蒸日上。

sentences.text: So if you're in the other 66 percent, like me, how do we get into the 33 percent?

translations.zh: 那么，如果你像我一样属于另外 66% 的人，那么我们如何才能进入 33% 的人呢？

sentences.text: Now I am a management consultant.

translations.zh: 现在我是一名管理顾问。

sentences.text: And so there's one thing that we love and believe deeply.

translations.zh: 因此，我们深爱并坚信一件事。

sentences.text: Say it with me if you know it.

translations.zh: 如果你知道的话跟我说吧。

sentences.text: What gets measured, gets managed.

translations.zh: 衡量什么，就管理什么。

sentences.text: Come on, guys, you know this.

translations.zh: 来吧，伙计们，你知道这一点。

sentences.text: You know, what is it, 50 percent of us use wearables to measure our health, to presumably manage it.

translations.zh: 你知道吗，我们 50% 的人使用可穿戴设备来衡量我们的健康状况，大概是为了管理它。

sentences.text: But I couldn't find anything like that to measure all the dimensions that mattered in life.

translations.zh: 但我找不到类似的东西来衡量生活中所有重要的维度。

sentences.text: So I created this super simple, kind of clunky Excel tracker that I use.

translations.zh: 所以我创建了这个超级简单、笨重的 Excel 跟踪器并使用。

sentences.text: So the first step was to identify the dimensions of a good life.

translations.zh: 因此，第一步是确定美好生活的维度。

sentences.text: So I started with my faith, but then I brought in insights from the thinkers who've studied this stuff.

translations.zh: 所以我从我的信仰开始，但后来我引入了研究过这些东西的思想家的见解。

sentences.text: So from Aristotle to this thing called the Harvard Happiness Study to Martin Seligman's positive psychology to Clayton Christensen’s “How Will You Measure Your Life?

translations.zh: 因此，从亚里士多德到哈佛幸福研究，到马丁·塞利格曼的积极心理学，再到克莱顿·克里斯滕森的“你将如何衡量你的生活？”

sentences.text: ” to my professor Arthur Brooks's research on happiness, to this global flourishing study and this cool article by my colleagues at BCG called "Strategize Your Life."

translations.zh: ” 我的教授阿瑟·布鲁克斯 (Arthur Brooks) 对幸福的研究，这项全球蓬勃发展的研究，以及我在 BCG 的同事撰写的这篇很酷的文章，名为“制定你的生活策略”。

sentences.text: And so I landed on these nine dimensions of the good life for me.

translations.zh: 因此，我找到了美好生活的这九个维度。

sentences.text: You're seeing these and you're like, pretty obvious, right which is kind of the point, because from the ancients to the moderns, these ingredients have been remarkably consistent.

translations.zh: 你看到这些，你会觉得，非常明显，这就是重点，因为从古人到现代，这些成分一直非常一致。

sentences.text: They're knowable.

translations.zh: 他们是可知的。

sentences.text: So step two, measure.

translations.zh: 所以第二步，测量。

sentences.text: I had the privilege of working for Gallup for a few years, if you know them.

translations.zh: 如果你认识的话，我有幸在盖洛普工作了几年。

sentences.text: And so I had this nerdy thing where I love building surveys.

translations.zh: 所以我有一个书呆子的东西，我喜欢建立调查。

sentences.text: So I turned these nine dimensions into kind of a 20-ish question survey that I answer pretty much every day.

translations.zh: 因此，我将这九个维度变成了一项包含 20 个左右问题的调查，我几乎每天都会回答这些问题。

sentences.text: Now, these are the questions.

translations.zh: 现在，这些就是问题。

sentences.text: I have a few others I answer weekly, like contribution to society that's sort of a weekly thing for me.

translations.zh: 我每周都会回答一些其他问题，比如对社会的贡献，这对我来说是每周都会做的事情。

sentences.text: My mom thinks it's daily, it's not.

translations.zh: 我妈妈认为这是日常，但事实并非如此。

sentences.text: But these to me basically are the picture of the good life to me.

translations.zh: 但这些对我来说基本上就是我美好生活的写照。

sentences.text: And so for the past 18 months, I've essentially answered these questions every day.

translations.zh: 所以在过去的 18 个月里，我基本上每天都在回答这些问题。

sentences.text: I do it at 9pm on my couch, on my phone, and it literally takes me 90 seconds.

translations.zh: 我晚上 9 点在沙发上用手机做这件事，实际上花了 90 秒。

sentences.text: But folks, this has been a total game changer for me.

translations.zh: 但是朋友们，这对我来说彻底改变了游戏规则。

sentences.text: My therapists have told me -- I have multiple therapists simultaneously which I hear is not best practice but here's what they agree on which is I have a lot of cognitive distortions.

translations.zh: 我的治疗师告诉我——我同时接受多名治疗师，我听说这不是最佳实践，但他们一致认为我有很多认知扭曲。

sentences.text: So, you know, these things, some of you have them, you're like, yeah, I've got some of those, too.

translations.zh: 所以，你知道，这些东西，你们中的一些人拥有它们，你会说，是的，我也有一些。

sentences.text: These are things like all-or-nothing thinking, catastrophizing, overgeneralizing.

translations.zh: 这些都是诸如“全有或全无”的思维、灾难性的思考、过度概括等。

sentences.text: Like when you pop up the news and you discover the sky is falling.

translations.zh: 就像当你弹出新闻时，你发现天塌下来了。

sentences.text: So for me, I was prepping for this TED talk a month ago, and I was already in pretty good shape, I thought.

translations.zh: 所以对我来说，一个月前我正在准备这次 TED 演讲，我想我的状态已经很好了。

sentences.text: And so I sent a recording of myself to some mentors, and I said, you know, I'd love some feedback.

translations.zh: 所以我把自己的录音发给了一些导师，我说，你知道，我希望得到一些反馈。

items.title: How to Power the World 24/7 — Without Oil | Cindy Taff | TED

translations.zh: How to Power the World 24/7 — Without Oil | Cindy Taff | TED

sentences.text: OK, so if you can't tell already, I'm from Texas.

translations.zh: 好吧，如果你还看不出来，我来自德克萨斯州。

sentences.text: And I also spent my career in the oil and gas industry.

translations.zh: 我的职业生涯也是在石油和天然气行业度过的。

sentences.text: So do you hate me yet?

translations.zh: 那你还恨我吗？

sentences.text: Let's hope not.

translations.zh: 我们希望不会。

sentences.text: So I went into oil and gas because energy is life.

translations.zh: 所以我进入了石油和天然气领域，因为能源就是生命。

sentences.text: And I wanted to be able to provide people with lights, air conditioning, heat, and, of course, the ability to watch funny cat videos on their phone.

translations.zh: 我希望能够为人们提供灯光、空调、暖气，当然还有在手机上观看有趣的猫视频的能力。

sentences.text: But energy also evolves.

translations.zh: 但能量也在进化。

sentences.text: So humans used to burn wood for heat and light.

translations.zh: 所以人类过去常常燃烧木头来取暖和照明。

sentences.text: We then transitioned to whale oil and to coal, and then to oil and gas.

translations.zh: 然后我们转向鲸油和煤炭，然后转向石油和天然气。

sentences.text: But we're now using renewables, like solar and wind.

translations.zh: 但我们现在使用可再生能源，如太阳能和风能。

sentences.text: And it's this exciting evolution to clean, renewable energy that inspired me to leave Shell and start my own geothermal company with my partners.

translations.zh: 正是这种向清洁、可再生能源的激动人心的发展激励我离开壳牌并与我的合作伙伴一起创办自己的地热公司。

sentences.text: And so we're now using that mud-on-your-boots know-how from the oil and gas industry to drill for heat instead of for hydrocarbons.

translations.zh: 因此，我们现在正在利用石油和天然气行业的专业知识来钻取热量而不是碳氢化合物。

sentences.text: So most people don't know this, but there's a lot of heat deep in the Earth.

translations.zh: 所以大多数人不知道这一点，但地球深处有大量的热量。

sentences.text: And the deeper you drill, the hotter it gets.

translations.zh: 钻得越深，温度就越高。

sentences.text: And this heat, this energy, it's always on, it's clean, and it actually holds 50,000 times more energy than all of the oil and gas reserves on the planet.

translations.zh: 这种热量、这种能量，它永远存在，它是清洁的，而且它所蕴藏的能量实际上是地球上所有石油和天然气储量的 50,000 倍。

sentences.text: So why am I still talking about oil and gas?

translations.zh: 那么为什么我还在谈论石油和天然气呢？

sentences.text: It's because we're going to take the technologies that the oil and gas have developed over the last 100 years, at a cost of trillions of dollars.

translations.zh: 这是因为我们将花费数万亿美元来采用过去 100 年来开发的石油和天然气技术。

sentences.text: And we're going to start drilling for heat instead of hydrocarbons.

translations.zh: 我们将开始钻探热量而不是碳氢化合物。

sentences.text: So let's talk a little bit about drilling, just to give you guys a basis on it.

translations.zh: 那么我们来谈谈钻探，只是为了给大家提供一个基础。

sentences.text: Oil used to be found in puddles on the ground.

translations.zh: 过去，石油是在地面的水坑中发现的。

sentences.text: So people figured out that they could use it to run machineries, cars, airplanes, and the demand skyrocketed.

translations.zh: 于是人们发现他们可以用它来运行机械、汽车、飞机，需求猛增。

sentences.text: The oil line on the ground ran out because people were using it.

translations.zh: 地面上的石油管道已经耗尽，因为人们正在使用它。

sentences.text: So we started to dig.

translations.zh: 于是我们开始挖掘。

sentences.text: So we dug deeper and deeper.

translations.zh: 所以我们挖得越来越深。

sentences.text: And despite what you may have heard or think about, you know, roughnecks and oil field work, it's actually quite complex and technical.

translations.zh: 尽管你可能听说过或想到过钻工和油田工作，但它实际上是相当复杂和技术性的。

sentences.text: The industry was able to advance the technologies even more to unlock oil that was previously out of reach, and at a cost the world could afford.

translations.zh: 该行业能够进一步推进技术，以世界可以承受的成本来开采以前遥不可及的石油。

sentences.text: And so, using amazing innovation, the industry learned how to basically turn the bit sideways in the subsurface, be able to drill horizontally within the layers that actually carried the oil and gas.

translations.zh: 因此，通过惊人的创新，该行业基本上学会了如何在地下将钻头横向转动，从而能够在实际携带石油和天然气的层内水平钻孔。

sentences.text: The industry also learned how to frack the rock to release oil that would have otherwise been stuck in there.

translations.zh: 该行业还学会了如何对岩石进行水力压裂，以释放原本滞留在其中的石油。

sentences.text: And so I know, not everybody's a fan of fracking, but it really did bring in an era of lower cost energy.

translations.zh: 所以我知道，并不是每个人都喜欢水力压裂，但它确实带来了一个更低成本能源的时代。

sentences.text: And I'll tell you in a minute why fracking is important for geothermal energy.

translations.zh: 稍后我将告诉您为什么水力压裂对于地热能很重要。

sentences.text: So the oil and gas industry, with all of this turning and steering underground, was able to drill deeper, drill hotter and actually drill with some pinpoint precision.

translations.zh: 因此，石油和天然气行业通过在地下进行所有这些转向和转向，能够钻得更深、钻得更热，并且实际上能够以一定的精确度进行钻探。

sentences.text: So, for example, imagine hitting a target the size of a pizza at a depth of five miles under the ground into the Earth.

translations.zh: 例如，想象一下将一个披萨大小的目标击中地下五英里深处的地球。

sentences.text: And being able to do this over and over again.

translations.zh: 并且能够一次又一次地这样做。

sentences.text: The technology is pretty amazing and so we are now going to use that technology for geothermal but we're not going to have to learn over those 100 years.

translations.zh: 这项技术非常惊人，所以我们现在将把这项技术用于地热发电，但我们不需要花 100 年的时间来学习。

sentences.text: So we can apply it straight away.

translations.zh: 所以我们可以立即应用它。

sentences.text: So you guys may ask, why do we even need geothermal because let's admit, wind and solar have done a great job greening the grid.

translations.zh: 所以你们可能会问，为什么我们甚至需要地热，因为我们承认，风能和太阳能在绿化电网方面做得很好。

sentences.text: Well geothermal can do what wind and solar can’t, and that is provide power 24 hours a day, seven days a week, regardless of what the weather is.

translations.zh: 地热能可以做到风能和太阳能所不能做到的，那就是每周 7 天、每天 24 小时提供电力，无论天气如何。

sentences.text: You can also use geothermal as a giant battery to store extra energy from wind and solar and to make those intermittent power resources baseload because we can then use that extra energy during the day when the sun is not shining and the wind's not blowing.

translations.zh: 您还可以使用地热作为巨型电池来存储来自风能和太阳能的额外能量，并使这些间歇性电力资源成为基载，因为我们可以在白天没有阳光和风的时候使用这些额外的能量。

sentences.text: And so where is the real prize for geothermal?

translations.zh: 那么地热能的真正价值在哪里呢？

sentences.text: So I think a lot of us know about Iceland or the geysers in California, where they've done a great job of exploiting the shallow pools of hot water that's just below the surface.

translations.zh: 所以我想我们很多人都知道冰岛或加利福尼亚州的间歇泉，他们在开发地表以下的浅热水池方面做得很好。

sentences.text: The challenge with those areas is that it's kind of a unique, unicorn geology.

translations.zh: 这些地区面临的挑战是它是一种独特的独角兽地质。

sentences.text: So the real prize in geothermal is in rock that’s deep, hot, so you're going to have to drill deeper.

translations.zh: 因此，地热能真正的价值在于又深又热的岩石，所以你必须钻得更深。

sentences.text: So we call this next-generation geothermal.

translations.zh: 所以我们称之为下一代地热。

sentences.text: And that's where we're going.

translations.zh: 这就是我们要去的地方。

sentences.text: And we're going there by drilling deeper, hotter and horizontal.

translations.zh: 我们将通过更深、更热和水平的钻探来实现这一目标。

sentences.text: And I would say that there are several companies that are racing toward this future.

translations.zh: 我想说，有几家公司正在朝着这个未来而努力。

sentences.text: Ours is one of them.

translations.zh: 我们的就是其中之一。

sentences.text: I'm proud to say that we are not just talking about it.

translations.zh: 我很自豪地说，我们不仅仅是在谈论它。

sentences.text: We’re actually in the field, drilling wells, building systems and proving that geothermal technology can work in unassuming places like Texas who had ever heard of geothermal in Texas?

translations.zh: 我们实际上在现场，打井，建造系统，并证明地热技术可以在像德克萨斯州这样不起眼的地方发挥作用，谁听说过德克萨斯州的地热？

sentences.text: It's there.

translations.zh: 它就在那里。

sentences.text: OK, I said I’d come back to the f-word, fracking, and why is it important to geothermal.

translations.zh: 好吧，我说了我会回到那个词，水力压裂，以及为什么它对地热很重要。

sentences.text: So in this deep, hot rock, there's not a lot of cracks for water to flow through and the way we get geothermal to work is to flow water through fracks to absorb the heat.

translations.zh: 因此，在这层又深又热的岩石中，没有太多可供水流过的裂缝，而我们利用地热的方式就是让水流过裂缝来吸收热量。

sentences.text: And then we use that water to carry the heat to the surface.

translations.zh: 然后我们利用水将热量带到地表。

sentences.text: And when you get that heat to the surface, you can use it to drive turbines, to power electricity, or you can use the heat directly.

translations.zh: 当你将这些热量带到地表时，你可以用它来驱动涡轮机、发电，或者你也可以直接使用这些热量。

sentences.text: The reason why we use fracturing is we can create those cracks in the rock, or those pathways through which the water can flow through.

translations.zh: 我们使用压裂技术的原因是我们可以在岩石中制造裂缝，或者水可以流过的通道。

sentences.text: So here is a picture of a frack operation.

translations.zh: 这是一张水力压裂作业的图片。

sentences.text: This was actually at our well so what we do during fracking operations is we are creating cracks in the rock or we're widening cracks that are already there.

translations.zh: 这实际上是在我们的井里，所以我们在水力压裂操作中所做的就是在岩石中制造裂缝，或者扩大已经存在的裂缝。

sentences.text: And we're using it with a liquid that is mainly water.

translations.zh: 我们将其与主要是水的液体一起使用。

sentences.text: We do add crushed rock called barite in the water to make it more dense.

translations.zh: 我们确实在水中添加了称为重晶石的碎石，以使其更加致密。

sentences.text: The thing I want to note is that we're pumping at lower pressures and lower rates than the oil and gas industry.

translations.zh: 我要指出的是，我们的抽油压力和速率比石油和天然气行业更低。

items.title: How to Raise Kids Who Can Handle Hard Things | Kathryn Hecht | TED

translations.zh: How to Raise Kids Who Can Handle Hard Things | Kathryn Hecht | TED

sentences.text: I've walked a lot in these shoes today.

translations.zh: 今天我穿着这双鞋走了很多路。

sentences.text: These soles have ground into the pavement of downtown Minneapolis, the rubber mats of my car, the linoleum of a gas station bathroom and the Play Doh-crusted carpet of a daycare.

translations.zh: 这些鞋底已经磨进了明尼阿波利斯市中心的人行道、我汽车的橡胶垫、加油站浴室的油毡和托儿所布满 Play Doh 结垢的地毯。

sentences.text: Embedded in the tread, a smear a toddler booger.

translations.zh: 胎面中嵌入了幼儿鼻屎的痕迹。

sentences.text: Yeah, a little leftover norovirus maybe.

translations.zh: 是的，也许还有一点诺如病毒残留。

sentences.text: Maybe if I'm really lucky, a little fleck of dog poop.

translations.zh: 如果我真的很幸运的话，也许会有一点狗屎。

sentences.text: Makes you sick just thinking about it, right?

translations.zh: 光是想想就让人恶心，对吧？

sentences.text: Audience: Ooh!

translations.zh: 观众：哦！

sentences.text: So, yeah, that happened.

translations.zh: 所以，是的，事情发生了。

sentences.text: And while you may not have tasted what I just tasted, you felt what I felt.

translations.zh: 虽然你可能没有尝过我刚刚尝过的味道，但你感受到了我的感受。

sentences.text: Your sympathetic nervous system activated, increasing your heart rate and tensing your muscles.

translations.zh: 您的交感神经系统被激活，心率加快，肌肉紧张。

sentences.text: Your anterior insula flared, creating a feeling of disgust.

translations.zh: 你的前岛叶张开，产生一种厌恶的感觉。

sentences.text: Little nausea, slight gag reflex.

translations.zh: 轻微恶心，轻微呕吐反射。

sentences.text: Am I going to get sick now?

translations.zh: 我现在会生病吗？

sentences.text: I don't know.

translations.zh: 我不知道。

sentences.text: But I do know this.

translations.zh: 但我确实知道这一点。

sentences.text: I am so glad you're uncomfortable.

translations.zh: 我很高兴你感到不舒服。

sentences.text: Congratulations, truly because that discomfort, that is the first essential step to creating confident kids.

translations.zh: 恭喜你，因为这种不适是培养自信孩子的第一步。

sentences.text: And you can trust me on this one.

translations.zh: 在这一点上你可以相信我。

sentences.text: I make kids uncomfortable for a living.

translations.zh: 我以让孩子们不舒服为生。

sentences.text: This week I had an eight-year-old stab me with a needle, twice, took a kid into a basement on a spider safari and played Uno on the bathroom floor with an understandably reluctant teen.

translations.zh: 这周，我被一个八岁的孩子用针刺了我两次，带着一个孩子去地下室进行蜘蛛狩猎，并在浴室地板上和一个不情愿的青少年一起玩乌诺，这是可以理解的。

sentences.text: It's only Wednesday.

translations.zh: 现在只是星期三。

sentences.text: Now I’m not doing this stuff because I’m an evil psychologist.

translations.zh: 现在我不做这些事，因为我是一个邪恶的心理学家。

sentences.text: I'm doing this because as a pediatric anxiety and OCD expert, I'm a professional bravery coach.

translations.zh: 我这样做是因为作为一名儿科焦虑和强迫症专家，我是一名专业的勇敢教练。

sentences.text: I'd like to tell you about a kid that I worked with years ago named Sammy.

translations.zh: 我想告诉你一个多年前和我一起工作的孩子的故事，他的名字叫萨米。

sentences.text: Sammy was this sweet little third-grade string bean who lived for adventure.

translations.zh: 萨米是一个可爱的三年级小四季豆，为冒险而生。

sentences.text: Bright, curious, optimistic, could tell you everything you honestly never needed to know about airport design.

translations.zh: 聪明、好奇、乐观，他可以告诉你关于机场设计的一切你根本不需要知道的事情。

sentences.text: But Sammy had a fear: bees.

translations.zh: 但萨米有一个恐惧：蜜蜂。

sentences.text: His brain appreciated bees, vital pollinators.

translations.zh: 他的大脑很欣赏蜜蜂这种重要的传粉媒介。

sentences.text: His body, however, reacted like they were flying yellow needles with some anger issues.

translations.zh: 然而，他的身体反应就像飞黄针一样，有些愤怒。

sentences.text: As soon as those leaves turned green, Sammy would initiate his own personal bee safety protocol.

translations.zh: 一旦这些叶子变绿，萨米就会启动他自己的个人蜜蜂安全协议。

sentences.text: No sweets outside, social distancing from the flowers, even staying inside during his family's cabin trips when Sammy got to me, he and his parents had tried everything to get rid of this anxiety.

translations.zh: 外面不准吃糖果，与鲜花保持社交距离，甚至在萨米来找我时，他和他的父母都想尽一切办法来摆脱这种焦虑。

sentences.text: Deep breathing, distraction.

translations.zh: 深呼吸，分散注意力。

sentences.text: No luck.

translations.zh: 运气不好。

sentences.text: They had also debated the fear endlessly.

translations.zh: 他们还就恐惧进行了无休止的争论。

sentences.text: His parents would reassure him, "You won't get stung."

translations.zh: 他的父母会向他保证：“你不会被蜇到的。”

sentences.text: Sammy reminded his parents they were not fortune telling wizards in four words: "How do you know?"

translations.zh: 萨米用四个字提醒父母，他们不是算命巫师：“你怎么知道？”

sentences.text: This phobia was stealing Sammy's childhood one sunny summer day at a time when I sing Happy Birthday with a cringing 12-year-old, as loudly as possible, in the grocery store produce section when I rank order photos of vomit by chunk level, or, yes when I lick my own dirty shoes, I don't just do it for fun although, believe it or not, sometimes it's very fun.

translations.zh: 这种恐惧症正在偷走萨米的童年。在一个阳光明媚的夏日，当我在杂货店农产品区与一个畏缩的 12 岁孩子尽可能大声地唱生日快乐歌时，我会按块级别排列呕吐物的照片，或者，是的，当我舔自己的脏鞋子时，我这样做并不只是为了好玩，尽管信不信由你，有时这很有趣。

sentences.text: I do it for kids like Sammy, because there is a method to this madness.

translations.zh: 我为像萨米这样的孩子做这件事，因为这种疯狂是有办法的。

sentences.text: And after a decade of clinical practice helping kids be brave, it's become clear to me that the method, exposure therapy, isn't just the gold-standard treatment for child anxiety and OCD.

translations.zh: 经过十年帮助孩子变得勇敢的临床实践，我清楚地认识到，暴露疗法不仅仅是治疗儿童焦虑和强迫症的黄金标准。

sentences.text: It is a secret parenting playbook for raising kids that thrive.

translations.zh: 这是一本培养孩子茁壮成长的秘密育儿手册。

sentences.text: I want to share that secret playbook with you today.

translations.zh: 今天我想与大家分享这本秘密剧本。

sentences.text: But before we talk about what to do, we need to talk about what we are up against.

translations.zh: 但在我们讨论该做什么之前，我们需要先讨论一下我们面临的挑战。

sentences.text: It's a wild, worried world out there, folks.

translations.zh: 伙计们，外面的世界是一个狂野、令人担忧的世界。

sentences.text: According to the National Survey of Children's Health, pediatric anxiety diagnoses rose by nearly 30 percent from 2016 to 2019.

translations.zh: 根据全国儿童健康调查，2016 年至 2019 年，儿科焦虑症诊断增加了近 30%。

sentences.text: And that was before COVID.

translations.zh: 那是在新冠疫情之前。

sentences.text: But you don't need stats or lists.

translations.zh: 但您不需要统计数据或列表。

sentences.text: You have felt this, because thanks to evolutionary biology, when kids get anxious, adults get anxious too.

translations.zh: 你已经感受到了这一点，因为由于进化生物学，当孩子们感到焦虑时，成年人也会感到焦虑。

sentences.text: Now I have two girls, and home is the hardest clinic that I have to work in when one of my little gals looks up at me with the big teary eyes, aka the mommy bat signal, my nervous system does the same thing that yours does.

translations.zh: 现在我有两个女儿，家是我必须工作的最艰苦的诊所，当我的一个小女儿用泪水汪汪的大眼睛抬头看着我时，也就是蝙蝠妈妈的信号，我的神经系统会做和你一样的事情。

sentences.text: It responds as though I have discovered that the kitchen is on fire.

translations.zh: 它的反应就好像我发现厨房着火了一样。

sentences.text: The amygdala, the watchdog in our brain, starts barking and the fight-or-flight system kicks in and adrenaline surges, and there is this instant magical transfer of distress.

translations.zh: 杏仁核，我们大脑中的看门狗，开始吠叫，战斗或逃跑系统启动，肾上腺素激增，痛苦的瞬间神奇转移。

sentences.text: Her emergency becomes my emergency.

translations.zh: 她的紧急情况变成了我的紧急情况。

sentences.text: And in an emergency, what do you do?

translations.zh: 在紧急情况下，你会做什么？

sentences.text: You rescue the child.

translations.zh: 你救救孩子。

sentences.text: Now I am proof that professional degrees do not make you immune to this.

translations.zh: 现在我证明专业学位并不能让你免受这种影响。

sentences.text: In my eldest daughter's four short years of life, I have become a one-woman emotional SWAT team more times than I can count.

translations.zh: 在我大女儿短暂的四年生命里，我无数次成为了一名单身女性情感特警队。

sentences.text: I've answered questions for my daughter when she clams up with a new adult.

translations.zh: 当我女儿对新成人保持沉默时，我曾为她回答过问题。

sentences.text: I've sacrificed my sleep and allowed our little human space heater into the big bed for the night.

translations.zh: 我牺牲了自己的睡眠，让我们的小型人体加热器在大床上过夜。

sentences.text: I have forfeited all privacy while peeing because even that closed bathroom door feels too far away.

translations.zh: 小便时我已经丧失了所有隐私，因为即使那扇关闭的浴室门也感觉太远了。

sentences.text: Now all of this is what I call parenting for comfort, and it is the single most natural and well-meaning and deeply flawed thing that we do.

translations.zh: 现在，所有这一切就是我所说的为了舒适而养育孩子，这是我们所做的最自然、善意却存在严重缺陷的事情。

sentences.text: In the anxiety treatment world, parenting for comfort has another name: accommodation.

translations.zh: 在焦虑治疗领域，为了舒适而养育孩子还有另一个名字：住宿。

items.title: Are You Spending Your Money Wisely? | Wolfgang Schnellbaecher | TED

translations.zh: Are You Spending Your Money Wisely? | Wolfgang Schnellbaecher | TED

sentences.text: I've got two questions for each one of you.

translations.zh: 我有两个问题要问你们每一个人。

sentences.text: Question number one.

translations.zh: 第一个问题。

sentences.text: How much money did you spend in your entire life so far that already takes a bit to estimate, right?

translations.zh: 到目前为止，你这一生花了多少钱，已经有点难以估算了，对吧？

sentences.text: And question number two, did you spend your money wisely if you hesitate now and are not so sure, it likely means you're not a good buyer, frankly because you have not even defined for yourself what good buying truly is.

translations.zh: 第二个问题，如果你现在犹豫并且不太确定，这可能意味着你不是一个好的买家，坦率地说，因为你甚至没有为自己定义什么是真正的好购买，你是否明智地花钱了。

sentences.text: And that -- sorry for the bitter truth -- despite your buying things pretty much every day.

translations.zh: 尽管你几乎每天都在买东西，但对这个痛苦的事实感到抱歉。

sentences.text: Good buying can have such a massive effect on your entire life.

translations.zh: 好的购买会对您的一生产生巨大的影响。

sentences.text: And through your lifetime, you will spend a fortune, if not several fortunes, considering we're in Dubai.

translations.zh: 考虑到我们在迪拜，在您的一生中，您将花费一大笔钱，甚至几笔钱。

sentences.text: Spending it more wisely will either make you have to spend less or, for the same amount, make your life more luxurious, comfortable, sustainable, fun or whatever else you want it to be.

translations.zh: 更明智地花钱要么会让你花更少的钱，要么花同样的钱，让你的生活更加奢华、舒适、可持续、有趣或任何你想要的东西。

sentences.text: And the good news is there are people you can learn it from.

translations.zh: 好消息是你可以向一些人学习。

sentences.text: As a procurement leader in BCG, I work with them throughout the world.

translations.zh: 作为 BCG 的采购主管，我在世界各地与他们合作。

sentences.text: High tech companies, auto manufacturers, American Big Pharma, Middle Eastern oil companies, from Swiss clock manufacturers to Middle Eastern mass producers.

translations.zh: 高科技公司、汽车制造商、美国大型制药公司、中东石油公司、从瑞士钟表制造商到中东大规模生产商。

sentences.text: They all have to buy a lot of things -- steel, spare parts, intellectual property rights, fruit.

translations.zh: 他们都必须购买很多东西——钢材、备件、知识产权、水果。

sentences.text: Just last week, I did a live auction for a Norwegian salmon farm for fish.

translations.zh: 就在上周，我为挪威一家三文鱼养殖场进行了现场拍卖。

sentences.text: My promise to you is if you learn from the combined knowledge of these professional procurement teams, you will be a good buyer for the rest of your life.

translations.zh: 我向您保证，如果您学习这些专业采购团队的综合知识，您将成为您一生的好买家。

sentences.text: And on top, it's easy.

translations.zh: 最重要的是，这很容易。

sentences.text: It only boils down to three rules these companies do right.

translations.zh: 归根结底，这些公司正确执行了三项规则。

sentences.text: Number one, prioritize on what really matters.

translations.zh: 第一，优先考虑真正重要的事情。

sentences.text: Now that sounds simple, doesn’t it?

translations.zh: 这听起来很简单，不是吗？

sentences.text: The truth is, it's not, because there's a few words missing.

translations.zh: 事实是，不是，因为少了几个词。

sentences.text: Prioritize on what really matters for a group, together.

translations.zh: 一起优先考虑对团队真正重要的事情。

sentences.text: In every company, in almost every household, there's a few players.

translations.zh: 每个公司，几乎每个家庭，都有一些玩家。

sentences.text: And even for yourself, you're oftentimes not so sure how to prioritize healthy food versus cheaper prices.

translations.zh: 即使对于你自己来说，你也常常不太确定如何优先考虑健康食品和便宜的价格。

sentences.text: How about a more comfortable buying experience versus sustainable products?

translations.zh: 与可持续产品相比，更舒适的购买体验怎么样？

sentences.text: Do we pay extra just to make our neighbor jealous?

translations.zh: 我们付额外的钱只是为了让邻居嫉妒吗？

sentences.text: You might laugh, but I'm from Germany, and you might argue we built an entire auto industry purely on that feeling.

translations.zh: 你可能会笑，但我来自德国，你可能会说我们纯粹基于这种感觉建立了整个汽车工业。

sentences.text: Now professional buyers, they actually read the corporate strategy, they align with business partners to find out what’s it really that makes our company better.

translations.zh: 现在是专业买家，他们实际上阅读了公司战略，他们与业务合作伙伴保持一致，以找出真正使我们公司变得更好的因素。

sentences.text: The superstar buyers go even further.

translations.zh: 超级明星买家走得更远。

sentences.text: They graphically pin down what are the priorities and then share with everybody.

translations.zh: 他们以图形方式确定优先事项，然后与所有人分享。

sentences.text: For your life, it might look like this.

translations.zh: 对于你的生活来说，它可能是这样的。

sentences.text: Maybe, at the start, during education, start of your career, you don't have so much money and therefore you want to have fun, but you have some cost consciousness.

translations.zh: 也许，在一开始，在教育期间，在你的职业生涯开始时，你没有那么多钱，因此你想玩得开心，但你有一些成本意识。

sentences.text: A bit later, you might form a family, [have] children.

translations.zh: 稍后，你可能会组建家庭，生孩子。

sentences.text: And you care a lot about quality of the food, of the toys, of the furniture, just to have it all right for them.

translations.zh: 你非常关心食物、玩具、家具的质量，只是为了让他们一切都好。

sentences.text: Maybe, a bit later, you care a lot about comfort.

translations.zh: 也许，过了一会儿，你会非常关心舒适度。

sentences.text: You just want to have an easygoing lifestyle.

translations.zh: 你只是想拥有一种轻松的生活方式。

sentences.text: I've even seen a lot of families like this, who have slightly different priorities.

translations.zh: 我什至见过很多这样的家庭，他们的优先事项略有不同。

sentences.text: Now the core thing is to be a good buyer, you don't have to follow a predefined set of rules.

translations.zh: 现在的核心是成为一个好的买家，你不必遵循一组预先定义的规则。

sentences.text: It's just for yourself and your loved ones, you need to have these priorities very clear.

translations.zh: 这只是为了你自己和你所爱的人，你需要非常清楚这些优先事项。

sentences.text: And now, we are already at rule number two.

translations.zh: 现在，我们已经处于第二条规则了。

sentences.text: Influence your allies.

translations.zh: 影响你的盟友。

sentences.text: Imagine the following situation.

translations.zh: 想象一下以下情况。

sentences.text: You're off to buy a new car.

translations.zh: 你要去买一辆新车了。

sentences.text: You arrive at the dealership, and the first thing that happens is your kids climb into the car.

translations.zh: 您到达经销店，发生的第一件事就是您的孩子爬上车。

sentences.text: They have dirty boots on and they yell and tell you how much they love it.

translations.zh: 他们穿着脏靴子，大喊大叫，告诉你他们有多喜欢它。

sentences.text: The colors, the design, everything.

translations.zh: 颜色、设计，一切。

sentences.text: You're already a bit on your back foot, starting to negotiate, and the car dealer basically tells you that right now, cars with dirty seats are a bit rare.

translations.zh: 你已经有点处于不利地位，开始谈判，汽车经销商基本上告诉你，现在座位脏的汽车有点罕见。

sentences.text: He can't do anything on the price.

translations.zh: 他在价格上无能为力。

sentences.text: In that moment, your wife gives you a look, like, "Please don't mess it up.

translations.zh: 在那一刻，你的妻子看了你一眼，好像在说：“请别搞砸了。

sentences.text: I also want this car so much."

translations.zh: 我也非常想要这辆车。”

sentences.text: You will very likely not buy the car.

translations.zh: 你很可能不会买这辆车。

sentences.text: You will for sure not buy it at a good price.

translations.zh: 你肯定不会以优惠的价格买到它。

sentences.text: In business, the good buyers, they know they have to align with their allies up front who's going to say what in a negotiation, after negotiation, before negotiation.

translations.zh: 在商业上，好的买家知道他们必须预先与盟友保持一致，他们会在谈判中、谈判后、谈判前说什么。

sentences.text: Only if these things are clear, they can ensure they really strike the best deals.

translations.zh: 只有弄清楚这些事情，他们才能确保真正达成最好的交易。

sentences.text: The superstar buyers go even further.

translations.zh: 超级明星买家走得更远。

sentences.text: They have the ability to influence the stakeholders on what they really want.

translations.zh: 他们有能力影响利益相关者，让他们知道他们真正想要什么。

sentences.text: Let me tell you about the inverting method.

translations.zh: 我给大家讲一下反转的方法。

sentences.text: I once had a workshop with a big railway company.

translations.zh: 我曾经在一家大铁路公司举办过一个研讨会。

sentences.text: We were supposed to reduce the cost of big steel tracks, and we had a group sitting there of rather old buyers basically telling us everything is optimal.

translations.zh: 我们应该降低大型钢轨的成本，我们有一群相当老的买家坐在那里，基本上告诉我们一切都是最佳的。

sentences.text: After 60 minutes, I was sitting in front of an empty flip chart, panicking a bit.

translations.zh: 60 分钟后，我坐在一张空的活动挂图前，有点惊慌。

sentences.text: My colleague, however, was very cool.

translations.zh: 然而，我的同事非常酷。

sentences.text: He just turned around to the crowd and said, "Let's forget this exercise for a moment.

translations.zh: 他只是转身对人群说：“让我们暂时忘掉这个练习吧。

items.title: What Losing Everything Taught Me About Resilience | Jane Marie Chen | TED

translations.zh: What Losing Everything Taught Me About Resilience | Jane Marie Chen | TED

sentences.text: Do you ever wonder who you are beyond your job, your titles, your accomplishments?

translations.zh: 你有没有想过，除了你的工作、你的头衔、你的成就之外，你是谁？

sentences.text: This is the question I was forced to confront when the company I'd spent a decade pouring my soul into shut down.

translations.zh: 当我花了十年心血投入的公司倒闭时，我被迫面对这个问题。

sentences.text: My work had been my entire identity, and without it, I didn't know who I was anymore.

translations.zh: 我的工作就是我的全部身份，没有它，我就不知道自己是谁了。

sentences.text: Ten years earlier, I had co-founded Embrace, a social enterprise that created a low-cost portable incubator for premature babies in underserved communities.

translations.zh: 十年前，我与他人共同创立了 Embrace，这是一家社会企业，为服务匮乏的社区中的早产儿创建了一款低成本便携式保温箱。

sentences.text: Our technology could work without constant electricity, making it usable in remote parts of the world.

translations.zh: 我们的技术可以在没有持续电力的情况下工作，使其可以在世界偏远地区使用。

sentences.text: We set an audacious goal: to save a million babies.

translations.zh: 我们设定了一个大胆的目标：拯救一百万婴儿。

sentences.text: I moved to India, where over 20 percent of all the world's premature babies are born, and I made that mission my life over the years, we saved thousands of babies.

translations.zh: 我搬到了印度，世界上超过 20% 的早产儿出生在印度，多年来我将这一使命作为我的一生，我们拯救了数千名婴儿。

sentences.text: Babies like Nathan, who was abandoned on a street weighing just two and a half pounds.

translations.zh: 像内森这样的婴儿，被遗弃在街上，体重只有两磅半。

sentences.text: He was rescued by an orphanage and kept inside our incubator for weeks.

translations.zh: 他被一家孤儿院救了出来，并在我们的孵化器里关了几个星期。

sentences.text: He survived.

translations.zh: 他活了下来。

sentences.text: Seven months later, I visited the orphanage and I held him in my arms.

translations.zh: 七个月后，我来到孤儿院，把他抱在怀里。

sentences.text: A few months after that, he was adopted by a family in Chicago.

translations.zh: 几个月后，他被芝加哥的一个家庭收养。

sentences.text: Stories like this kept me going.

translations.zh: 这样的故事让我继续前进。

sentences.text: Along the way, we were recognized by President Obama and funded by Beyonce.

translations.zh: 一路走来，我们得到了奥巴马总统的认可，并得到了碧昂丝的资助。

sentences.text: Our work was featured in headlines all over the world.

translations.zh: 我们的工作出现在世界各地的头条新闻中。

sentences.text: On the outside, it looked like a success story.

translations.zh: 从表面上看，这似乎是一个成功的故事。

sentences.text: But the truth was, on the inside, I felt like I was drowning in stress, exhaustion, self-doubt.

translations.zh: 但事实是，在内心深处，我感觉自己淹没在压力、疲惫和自我怀疑之中。

sentences.text: The work weighed so heavily on me, there were moments I felt like I could barely breathe.

translations.zh: 这项工作对我来说太沉重了，有时我感觉自己几乎无法呼吸。

sentences.text: Ten years in, after countless setbacks from manufacturing to distribution to funding challenges, we had to shut down the company.

translations.zh: 十年过去了，在经历了从制造到分销再到资金挑战的无数挫折后，我们不得不关闭公司。

sentences.text: I failed.

translations.zh: 我失败了。

sentences.text: I hit the lowest point of my life.

translations.zh: 我达到了人生的最低谷。

sentences.text: I was having panic attacks, I was depressed, I couldn't sleep, I felt completely broken in mind, body and spirit.

translations.zh: 我惊恐发作，心情沮丧，无法入睡，感觉思想、身体和精神都完全崩溃了。

sentences.text: So I decided to set off on a healing journey.

translations.zh: 于是我决定踏上治愈之旅。

sentences.text: I packed a surfboard and a suitcase

translations.zh: 我带了一个冲浪板和一个手提箱

sentences.text: And I bought a one-way ticket to Indonesia, where I threw myself into healing with the same intensity I'd once poured into my company.

translations.zh: 我买了一张去印度尼西亚的单程票，在那里我全身心地投入到治疗中，就像我曾经在公司里投入的那样。

sentences.text: I was willing to do anything because this was a matter of survival.

translations.zh: 我愿意做任何事，因为这事关生存。

sentences.text: I was going to heal the shit out of myself.

translations.zh: 我要治愈自己的伤痛。

sentences.text: I meditated for days in silence in the jungle until I hallucinated although I'm pretty sure those cockroaches on steroids were real.

translations.zh: 我在丛林里默默冥想了好几天，直到出现幻觉，尽管我很确定那些服用类固醇的蟑螂是真实的。

sentences.text: I did psychedelic journeys.

translations.zh: 我进行了迷幻之旅。

sentences.text: I dove with sharks so I could learn to relax.

translations.zh: 我和鲨鱼一起潜水，这样我就可以学会放松。

sentences.text: I don't know why I couldn't just get a massage like a normal person.

translations.zh: 我不知道为什么我不能像正常人一样接受按摩。

sentences.text: I burned holes in my leg for a frog poison ceremony.

translations.zh: 为了举行毒青蛙仪式，我在腿上烧了几个洞。

sentences.text: It was supposed to purge my past.

translations.zh: 它应该清除我的过去。

sentences.text: Instead, I think I purged everything I'd ever eaten in my entire life.

translations.zh: 相反，我认为我清除了我一生中吃过的所有东西。

sentences.text: But the real breakthroughs came only when I began to confront my childhood.

translations.zh: 但真正的突破只有在我开始面对自己的童年时才出现。

sentences.text: Growing up, my father showed his love by pushing me to excel.

translations.zh: 在我的成长过程中，父亲通过督促我追求卓越来表达他的爱。

sentences.text: I remember in second grade, on weekend mornings, I would cuddle with him and he would warm my cold feet under his as he quizzed me on my times tables.

translations.zh: 我记得二年级的时候，周末的早晨，我会拥抱他，他会用他的脚温暖我冰冷的脚，同时他会用乘法表来测试我。

sentences.text: Thanks to those drills, I won all the math competitions in my class when I didn't meet his expectations, I was punished.

translations.zh: 多亏了这些练习，我赢得了班上所有的数学比赛，当我没有达到他的期望时，我受到了惩罚。

sentences.text: Violently when I was 12 years old, I came home from school one day and I decided to read my history book on the front lawn.

translations.zh: 当我12岁的时候，有一天，我放学回家，决定在门前的草坪上读我的历史书。

sentences.text: It was a beautiful sunny day when my father came home and saw this, he flew into a rage.

translations.zh: 那是一个阳光明媚的日子，父亲回到家看到这一幕，勃然大怒。

sentences.text: He decided homework shouldn't be done on a lawn, it should be done at a desk.

translations.zh: 他认为家庭作业不应该在草坪上完成，而应该在桌子上完成。

sentences.text: And so he beat me.

translations.zh: 所以他打败了我。

sentences.text: And he demanded that I apologize.

translations.zh: 他要求我道歉。

sentences.text: I refused because for the first time in my life, I knew I had done nothing wrong.

translations.zh: 我拒绝了，因为这是我一生中第一次，我知道我没有做错任何事。

sentences.text: I also knew I was utterly powerless.

translations.zh: 我也知道我完全无能为力。

sentences.text: As I did this healing work, I finally connected the dots.

translations.zh: 当我进行这项治疗工作时，我终于把这些点联系起来了。

sentences.text: Feeling so powerless throughout my childhood had driven me to help the most powerless people in the world.

translations.zh: 我整个童年都感到如此无能为力，这促使我去帮助世界上最无能为力的人。

sentences.text: My pain had become my purpose.

translations.zh: 我的痛苦已成为我的目的。

sentences.text: But it had also become my shadow.

translations.zh: 但它也成为了我的影子。

sentences.text: No matter how many babies I saved or how much recognition I received, I never felt like I was enough.

translations.zh: 无论我拯救了多少婴儿，无论我获得了多少认可，我都从未觉得自己足够了。

sentences.text: Sometimes our trauma gets channeled into drive, perfectionism, overwork.

translations.zh: 有时我们的创伤会转化为动力、完美主义、过度劳累。

sentences.text: Some people numb their pain with substances.

translations.zh: 有些人用物质来麻痹疼痛。

sentences.text: I numbed mine with productivity.

translations.zh: 我的生产力让我麻木了。

sentences.text: I cared deeply about my work, but I also believe that my worth depended on what I achieved.

translations.zh: 我非常关心我的工作，但我也相信我的价值取决于我所取得的成就。

sentences.text: I finally stopped trying to achieve my way out of pain.

translations.zh: 我终于不再试图摆脱痛苦了。

sentences.text: Here's how I found my way back to myself.

translations.zh: 这就是我如何找到回归自我的方法。

sentences.text: First, I slowed down and I just let myself feel.

translations.zh: 首先，我放慢速度，让自己感受。

sentences.text: For most of my life, I had disconnected from my emotions to survive.

translations.zh: 在我生命的大部分时间里，我为了生存而脱离了自己的情绪。

sentences.text: Research shows most of us do this.

translations.zh: 研究表明我们大多数人都会这样做。

sentences.text: We avoid painful emotions through working, drinking, social media and other endless distractions.

translations.zh: 我们通过工作、饮酒、社交媒体和其他无休止的干扰来避免痛苦的情绪。
